seoTitle: Set Up and Customize Your HubSpot Dashboard
metaDescription: Build a custom HubSpot reporting dashboard by adding widgets, arranging the layout, applying filters, and managing team access permissions.
intro: A well-organized HubSpot dashboard pulls your key metrics into one view instead of hunting through separate reports. This guide covers starting from a template or blank canvas, adding report tiles, and arranging them with drag-and-drop controls. It closes with setting rolling date filters and managing who on your team can see or edit the dashboard.

steps.heading: Open the Dashboards directory
steps.body: Go to Reporting in HubSpot and access the Dashboards panel.

steps.heading: Start a new dashboard
steps.body: Click Create Dashboard and choose a template or a blank workspace to build from scratch.

steps.heading: Name it and set access
steps.body: Give your dashboard a descriptive name and configure initial visibility and team access rules.

steps.heading: Add report content
steps.body: Click Add Content to pull specific reports and metric tiles onto your dashboard canvas.

steps.heading: Arrange your layout
steps.body: Use drag-and-drop controls to resize rows, reorder charts, and position key metrics.

steps.heading: Apply filters and set as default
steps.body: Add rolling date ranges and object filters, then set the dashboard as your account default if needed.

faq.question: Can I control who sees my HubSpot dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, visibility and team access rules can be configured when you create or edit the dashboard.

faq.question: Can I add existing reports to a new dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, the Add Content option lets you pull specific existing reports onto your dashboard canvas.

faq.question: Does the dashboard update automatically?
faq.answer: Reports on the dashboard reflect live data, especially when rolling date filters are applied instead of fixed ranges.

faq.question: Can I set a dashboard as my default view?
faq.answer: Yes, the Actions menu lets you set a configuration as your account default landing view.
